data.body:
PASS 1 review (A1 — anti-slop / over-engineering / NIH). One finding (NIH / rebuilds-existing-artifact) plus affirmations.

AFFIRMATIONS (A1 sub-checks that PASS):
- YAGNI: the doc serves a CURRENT done-definition — the plan-review epic 5fd2's "Docs" AC and its "reuse rebar.llm extension points" / "reusing the close-gate signing machinery" ACs — not a hypothetical. Affirmed.
- No new abstraction (Rule-of-Three N/A): the plan introduces NO new code abstraction, interface, or wrapper. It is documentation-only ("This is documentation only — no behavior changes") describing existing seams. Affirmed.
- No config-surface proliferation: zero new config keys proposed. Affirmed.
- No golden-hammer / cargo-cult / resume-driven / premature-optimization / premature-abstraction: no new tool/pattern/tech is forced anywhere; the AC-7 anti-drift mechanism reuses the existing introspection-test approach rather than adding redundant tooling. Affirmed.

FINDING (A1 — NIH / rebuilds an artifact already in the codebase):

- [ ] Re-scope from "create a new doc" to "audit/verify the EXISTING doc for drift", because the proposed deliverable — `docs/reuse-surface.md` — and its supporting artifacts ALREADY EXIST and already satisfy AC1–AC6; authoring it fresh would duplicate production documentation.
  - location: plan "Why" ("there is no single developer-facing API REFERENCE … This ticket creates that reference") + Acceptance Criteria line 1 ("A developer-facing API reference exists (new doc under `docs/`, e.g. `docs/reuse-surface.md` …)").
  - criteria: A1
  - evidence:
    - The plan names its own target as a hypothetical "new doc"; that exact path is present today: `docs/reuse-surface.md`, titled "Reusable machinery — developer API reference", with §1 signing / §2 LLM workflow runtime / §3 prompt library / §4 output-schema seam — the same four sections this plan enumerates.
    - AC2 already satisfied: `docs/reuse-surface.md` §1 documents `sign_manifest`/`verify_signature`/`verify_record`/`signing_key`/`key_fingerprint`/`compute_signature`/`parse_manifest`/`head_sha`, the manifest format, HMAC-SHA256/`PAYLOAD_VERSION=1` canonicalisation, the `certified|mismatch|foreign_key|unsigned` verdict table, the `head_sha` staleness binding, last-writer-wins SIGNATURE replay, AND a "Worked example — a NEW gate reusing the surface" block (lines ~103–124) — the "not just the close gate" example AC2 asks for.
    - AC3 already satisfied: §2 documents the `Runner` protocol (run/preflight), every `RunRequest` field (mode/execution_mode/output_schema/extra_tools/…), `get_runner`, PydanticAIRunner/FakeRunner, and `run_workflow(...)` with step kinds + when-to-use guidance.
    - AC4 already satisfied: §3 documents `get_prompt`/`resolve_prompt`, the closed `FRONT_MATTER_KEYS` contract, `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` override resolution, the derived index + CI drift/parity/canonical gates, and "Adding a prompt"; §4 + `docs/output-schemas.md` document `register_contract`/`response_model_for`, `validate_structured`, packaged `src/rebar/schemas/*.schema.json`, and `OUTPUT_SCHEMAS`.
    - AC5 already satisfied: the existing doc's header states "Audience: human developers and LLM agents" and carries signatures + copy-pasteable code blocks throughout.
    - AC6 already satisfied: cross-linked at `docs/llm-framework.md:443` ("[reuse-surface.md] — the developer API reference…") and listed in `CLAUDE.md:8` ("reusable-machinery API reference `docs/reuse-surface.md`"). (Both cross-links resolve via grep today — note this contradicts earlier review comments that grepped them as absent.)
    - AC7 already satisfied: `tests/unit/test_reuse_surface_doc.py` EXISTS and is an `inspect.signature`-based anti-drift gate introspecting each documented callable (signing surface, runner/RunRequest, prompts, contracts, findings, workflow executor) — exactly the "AUTOMATED drift-check test … that introspects each documented callable" AC7 demands.
    - FP7 anti-FP (designated experiment/reference) checked and does NOT apply: the existing `docs/reuse-surface.md` is under `docs/` (not `docs/experiments/`), is not a `*_poc.*` / POC, and is already CITED BY PRODUCTION CODE (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:41`) and production docs (`docs/plan-review-gate.md:18,67,127`). So this is a rebuild of a production artifact, not the intended graduation of a reference/POC.
  - suggested_fix: Rewrite the "Why" to acknowledge `docs/reuse-surface.md` (+ `docs/output-schemas.md` + `tests/unit/test_reuse_surface_doc.py`) already exist, and re-scope the ticket from "create that reference" to "AUDIT the existing doc against current source for drift and fill any genuinely-missing AC item, then close." Change AC1 from "A developer-facing API reference exists (new doc…)" to "…is verified/extended". If a DIFFERENT additional doc is intended, the Why/Scope must name precisely what `docs/reuse-surface.md` is missing — as written the scope is indistinguishable from the existing file and would duplicate it.

This is the only A1 finding; the chunk is otherwise clean for this pass.
